ogTitle: Paynes Crossing vs Peacock Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Paynes Crossing and Peacock Creek.
heading: Paynes Crossing vs Peacock Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Paynes Crossing, New South Wales and Peacock Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Paynes Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Peacock Creek (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Paynes Crossing or Peacock Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Paynes Crossing has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Peacock Creek scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Paynes Crossing scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Peacock Creek scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Paynes Crossing or Peacock Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Paynes Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.23% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
